name: San Pedro Manrique from Magaña — circular via Santa Cruz de Yanguas and Castilfrío de la Sierra
name_original: San Pedro Manrique desde Magaña — circular por Santa Cruz de Yanguas y Castilfrío de la Sierra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Magaña, located in the Tierras Altas region of Soria, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by elevated terrain and river valleys. The area features a varied topography, including the Sierra de Alcarama and the Valle del Río Alhama, which provide diverse gradients for road cyclists. These routes often include scenic descents and challenging climbs, offering a physically engaging experience. The region's natural features are complemented by historical landmarks, enriching the cycling journey. Best road cycling routes around Magaña The most popular road cycling route is Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop, a 41.7 miles (67.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the historic Magaña Castle and explores the natural beauty of the Alhama Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular, a moderate 21.1 miles (34.0 km) path. This circular route navigates through the scenic landscapes around Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama. Local road cyclists also love the Tierra de Ágreda Loop via Cigudosa and Añamaza River Valley, a 46.8 miles (75.3 km) trail leading through the Añamaza River Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Magaña is defined by elevated terrain, river valleys, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Magaña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Magaña?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;14 road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Magaña. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from moderate to difficult, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Magaña?
A: The road cycling routes around Magaña are primarily categorized as &lt;em&gt;moderate&lt;/em&gt; or &lt;em&gt;difficult&lt;/em&gt;. There are 6 moderate routes and 8 difficult routes, reflecting the region's elevated terrain and varied gradients.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Magaña area?
A: Yes, many routes around Magaña are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077372286/magana-castle-and-alhama-valley-biosphere-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a more challenging circular ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Magaña?
A: Road cycling around Magaña is characterized by &lt;strong&gt;elevated terrain and river valleys&lt;/strong&gt;. You can expect a varied topography, including sections of the Sierra de Alcarama and the Valle del Río Alhama, which provide a mix of scenic descents and challenging climbs.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see along the routes?
A: The region is rich in historical sites. You can encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of la Nava del Marqués de Magaña&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views. Other notable sites include the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalebreras&lt;/a&gt; with its Gothic church, and the ancient fortified &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Blanca&lt;/a&gt; in Montenegro de Ágreda.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383532940/san-pedro-manrique-from-magana-circular-via-santa-cruz-de-yanguas-and-castilfrio-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Manrique from Magaña — circular via Santa Cruz de Yanguas and Castilfrío de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 97 km with significant elevation gain, provide a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Magaña?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the varied topography and elevated terrain suggest that &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn&lt;/strong&gt; would likely offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder weather to the 'Tierras Altas' (High Lands) region.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Magaña Castle?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077372286/magana-castle-and-alhama-valley-biosphere-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights views of the historic Magaña Castle. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383511516/navajun-from-valdemadera-circular-by-the-views-to-castillo-de-magana-and-cigudosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navajun from Valdemadera — circular by the views to Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa&lt;/a&gt;, also offers excellent perspectives of the castle.

Q: Can I find routes that explore river valleys in the area?
A: Many routes in Magaña incorporate river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077372286/magana-castle-and-alhama-valley-biosphere-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaña Castle and Alhama Valley Biosphere Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the natural beauty of the Alhama Valley, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1075667186/tierra-de-agreda-loop-via-cigudosa-and-anamaza-river-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Ágreda Loop via Cigudosa and Añamaza River Valley&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Añamaza River Valley.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available?
A: The longest road cycling route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383532940/san-pedro-manrique-from-magana-circular-via-santa-cruz-de-yanguas-and-castilfrio-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Manrique from Magaña — circular via Santa Cruz de Yanguas and Castilfrío de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 97.4 kilometers (60.5 miles).

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options around Magaña?
A: While the routes are generally moderate to difficult, some of the shorter, moderate circular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383520369/cigudosa-and-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cigudosa and Aguilar del Río Alhama Circular&lt;/a&gt; (34 km), might be suitable for families with older, experienced children, depending on their fitness levels and road cycling experience.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Magaña?
A: Over 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Magaña's varied terrain. While specific ratings are not available, the popularity indicates that the routes are well-regarded for their engaging physical challenges and the beautiful, diverse landscapes of the Tierras Altas region.
tips: Before the entrance to the town of Magaña there is an old bridge that crosses the Río Alhama and from where you can see the whole of the Castle that is at the top of the town.

Before reaching the town of Magaña, in one of the curves that descends to the Río Alhama, you can see the Castillo de Magaña and the whole of its town located at one of the meanders of the same river.

Population that is located to the south of the Sierra de San Cristrobal and that is crossed by the Río Linaris. The town, in the middle of the Iberian System, is located in the place called Tierras Altas.

Santa Cruz de Yanguas is a municipality in Soria in the Tierras Altas region, community of Castilla y León. During the Middle Ages it was part of the Community of Villa y Tierra de Yanguas.

It is located in the Baos River basin, and has important forest and mycological resources. The municipal area is part of the Urbión Regional Reserve, with a large presence of deer, roe deer, wild boar and woodcock.

Of its heritage, the Roman bridge that spans the bed of the Baos River, the church of the Santísima Trinidad (16th century) in Gothic style and the hermitage of the Virgen del Pilar stand out. As in the rest of the region, you can find dinosaur footprints (ichnites).
This small town in the Almarza region is located in the north of the province, bathed by the Chico river, a tributary of the Merdancho in the Duero basin, south of the Rodader mountain range

waypoints.name: Views of the Castillo de Magaña
